_comment: Server-authoritative quest SUB-PROGRESS config (docs/CONVERSION-CONTRACT.md §quest-graph companion). Validated by server/intent-quest.ts. These are the non-linear progress bits the generic quest-stage graph cannot express: sounding/diplomacy bitmasks (marks), kill counters/flags (kills), flexible OR/counted turn-ins (turnins), and quest-gated crafts/purchases (crafts). EVERY entry is gated by the parent quest being at the right stage, validated against SERVER state; a forged request FAILS or sets only the data-defined bit/grant. Sub-progress is stored in the owned quests map under its own key and reflected to the client via the IntentResult.questSet echo (monotonic).

marks._comment: A 'mark' sets a bit (bitmask) on a sub-key, gated by parentQuest being exactly at requireStage. order-independent soundings + diplomacy shuttles. `complete` (optional) advances the PARENT quest to `completeStage` once the bitmask reaches `completeMask`.

kills._comment: Server-driven kill credit (npcDeathHooks). When the killer's parentQuest is at requireStage and the counter sub-key is below `target`, the kill increments it. Fully authoritative: the client cannot forge a kill. The killer gets a {t:'intent', questSet} echo so the client mirrors the counter. `complete` (optional) advances the parent quest to completeStage when the counter reaches target.

turnins._comment: Flexible item turn-ins the AND-only graph cannot express. Advances parentQuest from requireStage to toStage, consuming `count` items drawn (in listed priority) from `anyOf`. The reward for `toStage` is then claimed separately via quest-reward.

crafts._comment: Quest-gated crafts/purchases: consume `inputs` (coins allowed; if present the craft is wealth-shaped and gated by ECONOMY_FROZEN) and grant `output`, gated by parentQuest at requireStage. Idempotency is NOT enforced (repeatable while inputs last) — these are mid-quest fabrications, not one-shot rewards.
